title: Werkflow Database Transport Adapter
description: Subschema for `spec.transport.config` and `spec.operations[].transportSpecific` when transport.type=database. Enforces architectural constraints: named queries only (no raw SQL in BPMN), parameterized binding (no string interpolation into SQL), read-mostly by default, view-scoped access recommended, hard row limits.

datasourceRef.description: Reference to a registered Werkflow datasource. JDBC URL and credentials live in the datasource registry, NEVER in the connector definition. The datasource is per-tenant; the same connector definition routes to different DBs across tenants.

dialect.description: Used by the engine to select dialect-aware features (e.g., FETCH FIRST vs LIMIT, RETURNING clause support).

readOnly.description: When true (default), the engine sets connection.setReadOnly(true) and rejects queries detected as DML/DDL at registration time. Setting this to false requires an explicit security review and enables write operations — discouraged. Workflow writes should go through REST APIs of the system that owns the data.

pool.description: Connection pool sizing for this connector. Independent of other connectors targeting the same datasource — prevents one slow connector from starving another.

circuitBreaker.description: Fail-fast behavior when the database is unhealthy. Prevents workflow threads from piling up against a dead DB.

queries.description: Named, parameterized queries this connector exposes. Operations reference queries by id. SQL is registered here ONCE by an admin/DBA — never composed dynamically from BPMN inputs.

schemaAllowlist.description: Optional. If present, the engine validates at registration time that every named query references only objects in these schemas. Belt-and-braces alongside DB-level grants. e.g., ['reporting', 'public'].

id.description: Stable identifier referenced from operations[].transportSpecific.queryRef. camelCase.

sql.description: Parameterized SQL using named parameters (`:paramName`). Parameter values are bound via PreparedStatement — string interpolation is rejected at registration time. The engine parses this for safety: presence of DML keywords (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE/TRUNCATE/DROP/ALTER/CREATE/GRANT) is rejected unless transportConfig.readOnly=false AND the query has a matching writeOperation: true flag.

parameters.description: Declared parameters bound to `:paramName` placeholders in `sql`. Order does not matter; binding is by name.

resultMode.description: How the result set maps into the operation's output. `object` = single row → object (errors if 0 or >1 rows). `array` = list of rows. `scalar` = single value (single row, single column). `void` = no result body (for write operations).

writeOperation.description: Must be true for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE statements. Combined with transportConfig.readOnly=false to actually permit writes. Two-key safety.

rowLimit.description: Hard upper bound on returned rows. Enforced at the JDBC layer via setMaxRows(). Workflows should not be ETL — if a customer needs >10K rows, that's a signal to redesign as a streaming integration.

queryTimeoutSeconds.description: Enforced via Statement.setQueryTimeout(). Default 5s — much shorter than HTTP defaults because workflow DB queries should be reference lookups, not analytics.

fetchSize.description: JDBC fetch size hint for streaming large result sets. Tune for DB driver: PostgreSQL needs autoCommit=false to honor this; Oracle's default is 10.

x-werkflow-keysetPagination.description: Required when the operation's top-level pagination.strategy=keyset. Keyset is the only sane choice for large tables; OFFSET-based pagination is a footgun and is not supported here.

name.description: Parameter name as it appears in `:paramName` in the SQL. Bound from operation input by exact name match.

type.description: JDBC binding type. Drives the PreparedStatement.setX call. `array-of-*` uses Connection.createArrayOf() — not all drivers support arrays; verify against your dialect.

default.description: Default value if the operation input does not supply this parameter. Type must match `type`.

maxLength.description: For string parameters. Enforced before binding to prevent oversized inputs from reaching the DB.

KeysetPagination.description: Keyset pagination uses the last row's key values as the cursor for the next page. The named query must be authored to accept these as parameters and apply them in a WHERE clause.

keyColumns.description: Columns that uniquely identify a row's position in the ordering. Typically the primary key, or (sortColumn, primaryKey) for non-unique sort columns.

ordering.description: Direction of iteration. Must match the SQL's ORDER BY direction.

cursorParameters.description: Parameter names corresponding to the previous page's last keyColumn values. The engine populates these on subsequent page fetches; the SQL applies them as `WHERE (col1, col2) > (:cursor_col1, :cursor_col2)` (or analogous).

pageSizeParameter.description: Parameter name controlling LIMIT / FETCH FIRST n ROWS. Bound from pagination.defaults.pageSize.

queryRef.description: References a query by id from transportConfig.queries[]. Validated at registration time — references to undefined queries reject deployment.

outputMapping.description: Maps result-set columns to output schema fields. Only needed when column names don't match the operation output schema property names. Default: identity mapping (column name = field name).

x-werkflow-emptyResult.description: Behavior when the query returns zero rows. For resultMode=object: `null` (default) sets the variable to null; `error` raises a NOT_FOUND mapped error. For resultMode=array: `empty-list` (default) sets variable to []; other modes are equivalent.
